description:
# Open question

Pre/post rollback semantics in detail, under the resolved §7/§8 model.

§8 failure order (settled): rollback prior plugins (reverse) FIRST, then discard the change worktree. Because plugins now mutate the worktree (§7) and discarding it already undoes their FILE changes, **rollback is about a plugin's EXTERNAL side effects** — tracker having pushed, jira having created a ticket — not about file state.

Open questions:
- The plugin gets one `rollback` op; the payload tells it which phase is unwinding (`pre` vs `post`, divided by the commit boundary). How does a plugin remember state from its `pre` run to its `post`/rollback run — env var, or a scratch-dir convention under `plugins/<name>/`?
- A plugin linked into `<op>/pre` but not `<op>/post`: does its pre side-effect get a rollback call when a LATER plugin's post fails?
- Idempotency / best-effort contract: balls will not retry and cannot verify rollback succeeded. Document it — best-effort, exit code ignored, plugin logs anything load-bearing to stderr.
- Provide a reference plugin showing the pre→post scratch-dir handoff.
title: Topic: pre/post hook rollback semantics
